blocks.text: Draft me a confidentiality clause for a Series B term sheet between a UK manufacturing company and a US lead investor. Standard market terms; mutual; 3-year survival.

blocks.thinking:
The lawyer wants a confidentiality clause for a Series B term sheet. This is a drafting task, not a document review task. Let me assess:

- **Task noun**: Confidentiality clause / term sheet
- **Relevant playbook**: None named for "confidentiality" or "term sheet" — the playbooks cover NDA and RFQ. Skip Step A.
- **Relevant skill**: None in the inventory specifically names "confidentiality clause drafting" — closest is perhaps general commercial drafting. No specific slug. Skip Step B.
- **Batch**: No batching. Skip Step C.

So I'm in "working without an attachment" mode — drafting in plain text.

Inputs needed:
1. Parties: UK manufacturing company (issuer) + US lead investor (investor)
2. Structure: Series B term sheet — confidentiality clause (mutual, 3-year survival)

I have strong priors on what a venture-term-sheet confidentiality clause looks like. I'll draft directly, highlighting my assumptions and offering to adjust.

Standard elements for a VC term sheet confidentiality clause:
- Mutual (both sides protect each other's confidential info)
- Definition of "Confidential Information"
- Exclusions (publicly available, already known, independently developed, etc.)
- Obligations (use only for evaluating the investment, protect with reasonable care)
- Survival: 3 years after disclosure (or termination) — lawyer specifies
- No obligation to disclose — typical carve-out

The lawyer said "standard market terms; mutual; 3-year survival" — that's enough to draft.

I'll produce a clean clause, mark assumptions, and ask if they need it in a particular format (standalone clause vs. full term sheet provision).

blocks.text:
Drafting a mutual confidentiality clause for Series B term sheet, 3-year survival. Assuming:

- **Both parties** bound equally (mutual)
- **"Confidential Information"** defined in the clause (not cross-referenced to a separate NDA)
- **Purpose** limited to evaluating and consummating the proposed Series B investment
- **Exclusions** standard — public domain, prior knowledge, independently developed, third-party right
- **Standard carve-outs** — permitted disclosures to advisors (under confidentiality), legally compelled disclosure
- **Survival** — 3 years from disclosure of each piece, or 2 years post-termination — lawyer specified survival length, I'll go with clause surviving termination

--- clause starts below ---

> **C. Confidentiality**
>
> **C.1 Definition.** "Confidential Information" means any non-public information disclosed by either party (the "Disclosing Party") to the other party (the "Receiving Party") in connection with the Proposed Investment, whether orally, in writing, or by any other means, and including all information contained in this Term Sheet and the exhibits and annexes hereto.
>
> **C.2 Exclusions.** Confidential Information shall not include information that: (a) is or becomes publicly available other than as a result of a breach of this clause C; (b) was already known to the Receiving Party prior to disclosure by the Disclosing Party, as evidenced by contemporaneous written records; (c) is independently developed by the Receiving Party without use of or reference to the Confidential Information; (d) is rightfully obtained by the Receiving Party from a third party without restriction on disclosure; or (e) is approved for release by prior written authorisation of the Disclosing Party.
>
> **C.3 Use and Protection.** The Receiving Party shall: (a) use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of evaluating and consummating the Proposed Investment; (b) protect the Confidential Information using the same degree of care it uses to protect its own confidential information, but in no event less than reasonable care; and (c) limit disclosure of the Confidential Information to those of its officers, directors, employees, agents, professional advisers, and potential co-investors who have a need to know such information for the purposes described in clause C.3(a) and who are subject to confidentiality obligations no less protective than those contained herein.
>
> **C.4 Permitted Disclosures.** Notwithstanding clause C.3, the Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information: (a) to the extent required by applicable law, regulation, or legal process, provided that the Receiving Party gives the Disclosing Party prompt written notice (to the extent legally permitted) and reasonably cooperates in seeking protective measures; or (b) to existing or prospective lenders, investors, or acquirers of the Receiving Party in connection with a bona fide financing or acquisition transaction, subject to a binding confidentiality agreement no less protective than this clause C.
>
> **C.5 No Obligation to Disclose.** Nothing in this clause C shall obligate either party to disclose any particular Confidential Information, or to enter into any further agreement regarding the Proposed Investment.
>
> **C.6 Return or Destruction.** Upon request by the Disclosing Party following termination of this Term Sheet or upon the consummation of the Proposed Investment, the Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy all Confidential Information (including all copies and extracts) and certify such destruction in writing, provided that the Receiving Party may retain one archival copy solely for legal and compliance purposes.
>
> **C.7 Disclosure of Existence.** Neither party shall disclose the existence or terms of this Term Sheet, or the fact that negotiations are taking place, except: (a) to the persons described in clause C.3(c); (b) as required by applicable law or legal process; or (c) to the Existing Shareholders of the Company, on a need-to-know basis.
>
> **C.8 Survival.** The obligations contained in this clause C shall survive for a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure of each item of Confidential Information, and shall remain in full force and effect following any termination of this Term Sheet.

--- clause ends ---

**Assumptions flagged:**

1. **"Proposed Investment"** is defined elsewhere in the term sheet — if not, recommend adding a definition in the header definitions.
2. **"Existing Shareholders"** assumed present in the cap table — if wrong, swap to "shareholders" or remove.
3. **No standstill** included — standard for some VCs but not others; if needed, this clause stays mute.
4. **Nofiduciary disclaimers** — US lead investors sometimes request; not included unless asked.

Want me to tweak the exclusions (narrower, broader), adjust the carve-outs, or strip any of the sub-clauses? Also happy to reformat as a standalone NDA cross-referenced in the term sheet, or flip to a one-way (US investor receiving, UK co keeping quiet) if that's the asymmetrical ask.

And a housekeeping note — the matter slug says "Negative control — generic confidentiality clause c17", but there's no attachment. Is this meant to replace an existing provision, or is this fresh drafting? Let me know and I'll align.
